core_payload.content:
# Agent Evals and Traces

## Dream Direction
In the Dream branch, every serious N1Hub agent lane should be traceable, replayable, and evaluable by design.

## Desired Future State
- each swarm cycle emits structured traces that can be replayed and diffed
- assignment-aware lineage replaces anonymous run blur
- reviewer and validation lanes score plans before promotion
- repeated failure modes are clustered and surfaced as engineering work
- canary task suites are used before changing models, prompts, or provider routing
- operators can see not only what an agent produced, but how reliable that pattern has been across time

## Why It Matters
Dream preserves the idea that long-lived agents should improve through evidence and feedback loops, not through folklore about which prompt felt smart last week.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Agent Evals and Traces in the Dream branch defines the future state where every important N1Hub agent lane produces replayable traces and structured evaluations. It imagines swarms that can be measured, compared, and improved through explicit canaries, assignment-aware lineage, failure clusters, and review scores rather than intuition alone. In that future, major runtime changes are judged against evidence from representative tasks instead of only by operator impression, memory, or operator mood.
